commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a new OpenAI-compatible completions compat option and DeepSeek/Xiaomi auto-detection intended to move volatile cache-boundary system-prompt suffix content into the first user message.

PR surface: Source +67. Total +67 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level failure path: current main keeps the dynamic cache-boundary suffix in the leading OpenAI-compatible completions system message, and the PR head does not pass the resolved new flag into that transport conversion path. No live DeepSeek cache-hit reproduction was run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor proof and maintainer choice among competing DeepSeek cache PRs; automation cannot supply the missing real-provider evidence for this external branch.

target.title: feat: add disableBoundaryAwareCache compat option for prefix-matching prompt cache providers
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

DeepSeek (and Xiaomi) use **prefix-matching prompt caching**: the entire system prompt prefix must be byte-identical across requests for cache hits. OpenClaw 6.x introduced boundary-aware caching (`<!-- OPENCLAW_CACHE_BOUNDARY -->`) that splits the system prompt into a stable prefix and dynamic suffix (project context files, session info, etc.).

For Anthropic/OpenAI which use explicit `cache_control` markers on specific message parts, this split works correctly — the stable prefix gets the cache marker and the dynamic suffix doesn't. But for DeepSeek, the **dynamic suffix variation invalidates the entire prefix match**, collapsing cache hit rate from **~95% to <10%** (~10x token cost increase).

## Why This Change Was Made

This PR adds a `disableBoundaryAwareCache` compat option. When enabled:

1. The system prompt is split at the cache boundary
2. Only the **stable prefix** is sent as the system message (byte-identical across turns → cache hits)
3. The **dynamic suffix** is prepended to the first user message as context (no longer breaks cache)

The option is **auto-detected** for `deepseek` and `xiaomi` providers. It can also be explicitly configured:

```yaml
# openclaw.json - model compat
compat:
  disableBoundaryAwareCache: true   # force enable for any provider
  disableBoundaryAwareCache: false  # force disable (e.g. if provider adds cache_control support)
```

## User Impact

- **DeepSeek users**: prompt cache hit rate should return to ~95% after this change, eliminating the ~10x cost increase experienced after upgrading to 6.x
- **All other providers**: no behavior change (default `disableBoundaryAwareCache: false`)
- **Anthropic/OpenAI cache**: unaffected (boundary-aware caching continues to work via `cache_control` markers)

## Evidence

### Typecheck
```
$ npx tsc --noEmit --strict src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts
TypeScript: No errors found

$ npx tsc --noEmit --strict src/config/types.models.ts packages/llm-core/src/types.ts src/agents/sessions/model-registry.ts
TypeScript: No errors found
```

### Tests
```
$ pnpm test src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  27 passed (27)

$ pnpm test src/agents/system-prompt-cache-boundary.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  10 passed (10)

$ pnpm test src/agents/anthropic-payload-policy.test.ts src/agents/openai-completions-compat.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  11 passed (11)
```

### Files Changed
- `packages/llm-core/src/types.ts` — add `disableBoundaryAwareCache?: boolean` to `OpenAICompletionsCompat`
- `src/config/types.models.ts` — add field to `SupportedOpenAICompatFields` pick
- `src/agents/sessions/model-registry.ts` — add field to JSON schema
- `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts` — auto-detect for deepseek/xiaomi; implement boundary split + dynamic-to-user-message logic in `convertMessages()`
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts` — preserve boundary when `disableBoundaryAwareCache` is enabled in `buildOpenAICompletionsParams()`

Fixes #94518. Related: #91018, #90583.
